item.title: 【Reliability Is Security: Why SRE Teams Are Becoming the Frontline of Cloud Defense】可靠性就是安全：为什么 SRE 团队正在成为云防御的前线
item.description:
【

Cloud operations have entered a strange new phase. The distinction between keeping systems running and keeping them secure has vanished. What looks like a reliability problem often turns out to be a security issue in disguise, and vice versa. Teams managing uptime are now, whether they planned for it or not, managing defense.

This shift didn't happen because someone decided it should. It happened because modern infrastructure forced it. The evidence sits in incident reports from the past eighteen months — outages caused by security tools, breaches that first appeared as performance problems, and configuration mistakes that somehow managed to be both at once.

】

云运营已进入一个奇怪的新阶段。保持系统运行和保持系统安全之间的区别已经消失。看似可靠性问题往往实际上是变相的安全问题，反之亦然。管理正常运行时间的团队现在正在管理防御，无论他们是否计划这样做。

这种转变之所以没有发生，是因为有人认为它应该发生。它的发生是因为现代基础设施迫使它发生。证据存在于过去十八个月的事件报告中——安全工具造成的中断、最初表现为性能问题的违规行为，以及不知何故同时出现的配置错误。

item.title: 【Building a Video Evidence Layer: Moment Indexing With Timecoded Retrieval】构建视频证据层：使用时间编码检索进行时刻索引
item.description:
【

Video has become a default knowledge source in many organizations. Whether it is trainings, internal demos, walkthroughs, webinars, or support screen recordings,  most of the times, video is the only place where a procedure was ever explained end-to-end. It's fine, until we need one step in the video again, not the whole video, just one step. Our requirement in that moment isn't a summary of the video; it is: 'Tell me what to do, and show me exactly where it happens. 

Most systems still treat video as a linear timeline, and timelines are fundamentally difficult to query. Even when you find the right section, it is hard to verify and share. Text search solved this for documents by making retrieval direct and citeable. Video is harder. Chapters and transcripts help with navigation, but they do not reliably answer the core question: given a query, locate the exact segment that supports the answer and cite it.

】

视频已成为许多组织的默认知识来源。无论是培训、内部演示、演练、网络研讨会还是支持屏幕录制，大多数时候，视频是唯一对过程进行端到端解释的地方。没关系，直到我们再次需要视频中的一步，而不是整个视频，只是一步。我们当时的要求不是视频的摘要；而是视频的摘要。它是：'告诉我要做什么，并告诉我到底发生在哪里。 

大多数系统仍然将视频视为线性时间线，而时间线从根本上来说很难查询。即使您找到了正确的部分，也很难验证和共享。文本搜索通过直接检索和可引用解决了文档的这个问题。视频比较难。章节和文字记录有助于导航，但它们不能可靠地回答核心问题：给定一个查询，找到支持答案的确切片段并引用它。

item.title: 【Kubernetes Autoscaling: What Breaks Under Real Traffic】Kubernetes 自动扩展：真实流量下的破坏情况
item.description:
【

Kubernetes autoscaling looks straightforward on paper.

Define resource requests.
Set up the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA).
Choose CPU or custom metrics.
Let the cluster scale automatically.

】

Kubernetes 自动缩放在纸面上看起来很简单。

定义资源请求。
设置 Pod 水平自动缩放器 (HPA)。
选择 CPU 或自定义指标。
让集群自动扩展。

item.title: 【Designing Production-Grade AI Tools: Why Architecture Matters More Than Models】设计生产级人工智能工具：为什么架构比模型更重要
item.description:
【

Why AI Tools Often Break Outside the Lab

AI has become one of the most accessible technologies in recent years. With the rapid release of coding models and managed AI services, non-developers are now building AI-based SaaS tools. Many of these tools solve real-world problems and, at least initially, work quite well.

Where things usually start to break down is not in the solution’s intelligence, but in the architecture that supports it.

】

为什么人工智能工具经常在实验室外崩溃

人工智能已成为近年来最容易获得的技术之一。随着编码模型和托管 AI 服务的快速发布，非开发人员现在正在构建基于 AI 的 SaaS 工具。其中许多工具可以解决现实世界的问题，并且至少在最初阶段工作得很好。

事情通常开始崩溃的地方不是解决方案的智能，而是支持它的架构。

item.title: 【When AI Crashes: Classifying Failure Modes in Safety-Critical Software】当人工智能崩溃时：对安全关键软件中的故障模式进行分类
item.description:
【

It is dangerous to treat AI systems like any other type of software development. While the code may run properly, the model may still have 99% confidence that a kangaroo is a pedestrian.

AI systems can be broken down into two types of failures; perception failures and planning failures. These failures are difficult to determine because they do not create error messages that a developer would see while developing the application. Instead of returning an error message stating that the model does not understand the input, the model could return a prediction such as "speed limit 45 mph".

】

像任何其他类型的软件开发一样对待人工智能系统是危险的。虽然代码可以正常运行，但模型仍然有 99% 的置信度认为袋鼠是行人。

人工智能系统的故障可以分为两种类型：感知失败和计划失败。这些故障很难确定，因为它们不会创建开发人员在开发应用程序时看到的错误消息。该模型可以返回诸如“速度限制 45 英里/小时”之类的预测，而不是返回表明模型无法理解输入的错误消息。

item.title: 【Memory Optimization and Utilization in Java 25 LTS: Practical Best Practices】Java 25 LTS 中的内存优化和利用：实用最佳实践
item.description:
【

Memory tuning in Java has evolved over years and whenever each version was released, we anticipate some magic. If you worked with Java 6 or 7, you probably remember spending hours tweaking PermGen, experimenting with CMS flags, and nervously watching GC logs in production. But with Java 25, Memory Optimization and Utilization are more mature.

Modern Java gives us better garbage collectors, improved container awareness, stronger tooling, and smarter runtime ergonomics. But despite all that progress, memory optimization is something that you can't ignore. In a cloud-native environment where every gigabyte costs money, memory efficiency directly affects both performance and money spent on infrastructure as well.

In this article I am trying to summarize some of the best practices for memory utilization, so developers can use it as a reference guide.

1. Start with Measurement, Not Assumptions

The most common mistake that we could usually see is increasing heap size without understanding allocation patterns. A bigger heap often delays a problem rather than solving it.

】

Java 中的内存调整已经发展了多年，每当每个版本发布时，我们都会期待一些魔法。如果您使用过 Java 6 或 7，您可能还记得花费数小时调整 PermGen、试验 CMS 标志以及紧张地观察生产中的 GC 日志。但在 Java 25 中，内存优化和利用率更加成熟。

现代 Java 为我们提供了更好的垃圾收集器、改进的容器感知、更强大的工具和更智能的运行时人体工程学。但尽管取得了这些进展，内存优化仍然是您不能忽视的事情。在每 GB 都需要花钱的云原生环境中，内存效率直接影响性能和基础设施支出。

在本文中，我试图总结一些内存利用的最佳实践，以便开发者可以将其用作参考指南。

1.从测量开始，而不是假设

我们通常会看到的最常见错误是在不了解分配模式的情况下增加堆大小。更大的堆通常会延迟问题而不是解决问题。

item.title: 【Edge + GenAI: The Architecture Behind Instant Digital Experiences】Edge + GenAI：即时数字体验背后的架构
item.description:
【

Real time digital experiences are no longer dictated by cloud strategy alone. They’re being shaped at the edge, where decisions happen in real time, not after-the-fact reporting. 

In the moments that matter, milliseconds make or break the experience. They decide whether payments clear, checkouts stall, issues get contained early, or customers drop off entirely. 

】

实时数字体验不再仅由云策略决定。它们是在边缘形成的，决策是实时发生的，而不是事后报告。 

在关键时刻，毫秒决定体验的成败。他们决定付款是否顺利、结账是否停止、问题是否得到提早遏制，或者客户是否完全流失。

item.title: 【Shipping GenAI Into an Existing App: How to Integrate AI Features Without Rewriting Your Stack】将 GenAI 集成到现有应用程序中：如何在不重写堆栈的情况下集成 AI 功能
item.description:
【

Editor’s Note: The following is an article written for and published in DZone’s 2026 Trend Report, Generative AI: From Prototypes to Production, Operationalizing AI at Scale.

Most production applications already have an architecture, release process, and users who depend on predictable behavior. While adding a generative AI (GenAI) feature into an existing app doesn’t change any of that, it introduces uncertainties like non-deterministic outputs, increased latency due to model responses, and failure modes that look like successes but contain hallucinated responses. We tend to treat AI as a special case; however, the goal isn’t to build an AI platform but to ship a bounded feature that can improve a specific workflow without creating technical debt across the codebase.

】

编者注：以下是为 DZone 2026 年趋势报告撰写并发表的文章，生成式 AI：从原型到生产，大规模实施人工智能。

<小时>

大多数生产应用程序已经拥有架构、发布流程以及依赖可预测行为的用户。虽然在现有应用程序中添加生成式人工智能 (GenAI) 功能不会改变任何这些，但它会带来不确定性，例如不确定性输出、模型响应导致的延迟增加，以及看似成功但包含幻觉响应的失败模式。我们倾向于将人工智能视为一个特例；然而，我们的目标不是构建一个人工智能平台，而是提供一个有限的功能，该功能可以改进特定的工作流程，而不会在代码库中产生技术债务。

item.title: 【Building a Deterministic Event Correlation Engine in Go for High-Volume Alert Systems】在 Go 中为大容量警报系统构建确定性事件关联引擎
item.description:
【

High-volume alert systems are deceptively complex. At small scale, correlating alerts into incidents feels straightforward: group similar events within a time window and move on. But as throughput increases and distributed systems enter the picture, subtle sources of nondeterminism begin to creep in.

Events arrive out of order. Clocks drift. Network latency fluctuates. Goroutine scheduling changes across runs. Even Go's randomized map iteration can alter decision paths. The result? The same set of alerts can produce different incident groupings depending on timing and execution order.

】

大容量警报系统看似复杂。在小规模情况下，将警报与事件相关联感觉很简单：将时间窗口内的类似事件分组并继续。但随着吞吐量的增加和分布式系统的出现，不确定性的微妙来源开始悄然出现。

事件到达时没有顺序。时钟漂移。网络延迟波动。 Goroutine 调度在运行过程中会发生变化。甚至 Go 的随机地图迭代也可以改变决策路径。结果呢？根据时间和执行顺序，同一组警报可能会产生不同的事件分组。

item.title: 【Getting Started with Gemini Agents: Build a Data-Connected RAG Agent using Vertex AI Agent Builder】Gemini Agent 入门：使用 Vertex AI Agent Builder 构建数据连接的 RAG 代理
item.description:
【

Generative AI has shifted from simple chat interfaces to complex, autonomous agents that can reason, plan, and — most importantly — access private data. While large language models (LLMs) like Gemini are incredibly capable, they are limited by their knowledge cutoff and lack of access to your specific business data.

This is where Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) comes in. RAG allows an LLM to retrieve relevant information from a trusted data source before generating a response. However, building a RAG pipeline from scratch — handling vector databases, embeddings, chunking, and ranking — can be a daunting task.

】

生成式人工智能已经从简单的聊天界面转变为复杂的自主代理，可以推理、计划，并且最重要的是访问私人数据。虽然像 Gemini 这样的大型语言模型 (LLM) 的能力令人难以置信，但它们受到知识局限性和无法访问特定业务数据的限制。

这就是检索增强生成 (RAG) 的用武之地。RAG 允许 LLM 在生成响应之前从可信数据源检索相关信息。然而，从头开始构建 RAG 管道（处理向量数据库、嵌入、分块和排名）可能是一项艰巨的任务。
